title: fabaceae
intro: The Fabaceae rank, also known as the legume family, is a large and diverse group of plants that includes a wide range of flowering plants commonly known as beans, peas, and lentils. Characterized by their complex fruit structures and distinctive seed pods, Fabaceae members are found in various environments around the world, from deserts to tropical rainforests. This family includes approximately 19,000 known species, many of which are economically important as food sources or have significant cultural and ecological roles. Fabaceae plants are often nitrogen-fixing, making them valuable for soil health and agricultural sustainability.
